title: `docker pull` fails with `layers from manifest don't match image configuration` on `tar.xz` imported images published by `podman`
body:
### Issue Description

`podman import` from `tar.xz` archive, followed by `podman push` results in image that cannot be pulled with `docker`. Works fine with `podman` though.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Start podman container and run `dockerd`:
`podman run -u root --rm -i -t --name docker-test --privileged quay.io/podman/stable bash -c "dnf install -y docker && dockerd"`
2. In another shell start local docker repository:
`podman exec -i -t -u podman docker-test podman run -d -p 5000:5000 registry:2`
3. Configure new registry:
```
podman exec -i -u podman docker-test bash -c 'cat > $HOME/.config/containers/registries.conf' <<EOF
[[registry]]  
insecure=true
location="localhost:5000"
EOF
```
4. Prepare dummy archive:
`podman exec -i -t -u podman docker-test bash -c "mkdir /tmp/test; echo test > /tmp/test/test; tar -Jcf /tmp/test/test.tar.xz -C /tmp/test test"`
5. Import dummy image:
`podman exec -i -t -u podman docker-test podman import /tmp/test/test.tar.xz localhost:5000/test`
6. Push new image:
`podman exec -i -t -u podman docker-test podman push localhost:5000/test`
7. Remove local image to confirm pull works fine:
` podman exec -i -t -u podman docker-test podman rmi localhost:5000/test`
8. Run `podman pull` to verify if it works with `podman`:
`podman exec -i -t -u podman docker-test podman pull localhost:5000/test`
9. Run `docker pull` to verify if it works with `docker`:
`podman exec -i -t -u root docker-test docker pull localhost:5000/test`



### Describe the results you received

For `podman` it works fine:
```
Trying to pull localhost:5000/test:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob d195f80ed8a3 done  
Copying config 1cb39c4052 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
1cb39c4052dd909b352e9b081de0428c091bdaf4b22b68baf9e9711ce0a92722
```

However with `docker` it fails:
```
Using default tag: latest
latest: Pulling from test
d195f80ed8a3: Pull complete 
layers from manifest don't match image configuration
```

It appears to be related to `tar.xz` input, since it's not reproducible for `tar.gz`.

### Describe the results you expected

Works the same for both `podman` and `docker`.

### podman info output

```yaml

```


### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
That's an issue with https://github.com/containers/image/. While other parts of `podman` do support `tar.xz` tarball, this part doesn't:
https://github.com/containers/image/blob/72e00bc1ceabfe2cb76d9801fc2ecfbab5d23aca/tarball/tarball_src.go#L85-L103

As a result image ends up with incorrect:
```json
          "RootFS": {
               "Type": "layers",
               "Layers": [
                    "sha256:2717cb4b5870c4afca0ddaa9e194b8bb31c4aade7151cf336a4409551291cc6b"
               ]
          },
```
Which contains digest of `tar.xz` instead of uncompressed `tar`.

Would be good if `containers/image` could share logic with `containers/storage`:
https://github.com/containers/storage/blob/6461b51e4dfb07bb7e756605c5dfa27b7a48bb33/pkg/archive/archive.go#L182-L224
